content.full_text:
August 27, 1991

Lynda Cassady
Political Reform Consultant
California Fair Political
    Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804-0807


Dear Ms. Cassady,

    I may be a candidate next year, so I would like some
advise to avoid any problems as the reporting requirements.

    The instructions in use in 1986 for preparing Schedule E
included this statement: (Copy enclosed)

    " The deposit of campaign funds into an interest
bearing account, or the purchase of certificates
of deposit, stock, shares in a mutual bond or any
other asset which can be readily converted to
cash, should not be reported as an expenditure,
and the committee's cash on hand should not be
reduced by the amount of the investment. These
amounts should be included in the "cash on hand"
figure."

    I do not find a similar statement in the current
instructions in regard to purchasing stock or any other asset
that can be readily converted to cash.

QUESTION 1:

    May I transfer campaign funds from a bank account into a
stock market fund sold by such firms as Merrill Lynch Pierce
Fenner and Smith Inc. or Bateman Eichler Hill and Richards Inc.
without violating section 85201?

QUESTION 2:

    I have reviewed the Political Reform Act of 1974 and I
haven't found any prohibition against a candidate paying for
various political activities and then being reimbursed from his
campaign fund. Is there such a prohibition? If so, please
advise me which section applies.

QUESTION 3:

    May I use a credit card to pay for various campaign
related expenses and then pay the credit card company from
campaign funds?

August 27, 1991
Lynda Cassady
page 2


Question 4:

     If I list an expenditure for travel, do I have to list the
destination and purpose of the trip?

Sincerely,

James C Griffin

James C. Griffin
5069 Bain Street,
Mira Loma, California 91752

Box 807 Sacramento, California 95804-0807 Dear Ms. Cassady, I may be a candidate next year, so I would like some advise to avoid any problems as the reporting requirements. The instructions in use in 1986 for preparing Schedule E included this statement: (Copy enclosed) " The deposit of campaign funds into an interest bearing account, or the purchase of certificates of deposit, stock, shares in a mutual bond or any other asset which can be readily converted to cash, should not be reported as an expenditure, and the committee's cash on hand should not be reduced by the amount of the investment. These amounts should be included in the "cash on hand" figure." I do not find a similar statement in the current instructions in regard to purchasing stock or any other asset that can be readily converted to cash. QUESTION 1: May I transfer campaign funds from a bank account into a stock market fund sold by such firms as Merrill Lynch Pierce Fenner and Smith Inc. or Bateman Eichler Hill and Richards Inc. without violating section 85201? QUESTION 2: I have reviewed the Political Reform Act of 1974 and I haven't found any prohibition against a candidate paying for various political activities and then being reimbursed from his campaign fund. Is there such a prohibition? If so, please advise me which section applies. QUESTION 3: May I use a credit card to pay for various campaign related expenses and then pay the credit card company from campaign funds? August 27, 1991 Lynda Cassady page 2 Question 4: If I list an expenditure for travel, do I have to list the destination and purpose of the trip? Sincerely, James C Griffin James C.
